divergences.description: CAPABILITY_MANIFEST.json declares GCS path gs://madhav-marsys-sources/L1/structured/CHART_FACTS_EXTRACTION_v1_0.yaml but the object actually lives at gs://madhav-marsys-sources/L1/facts/STRUCTURED/CHART_FACTS_EXTRACTION_v1_0.yaml
divergences.notes: File exists at actual path; sha256 confirmed match in Stream B. Manifest entry is incorrect — path registered under wrong GCS prefix.

divergences.description: CAPABILITY_MANIFEST.json declares GCS path gs://madhav-marsys-sources/L1/structured/CHART_FACTS_SCHEMA_v1_0.json but the object actually lives at gs://madhav-marsys-sources/L1/facts/STRUCTURED/CHART_FACTS_SCHEMA_v1_0.json
divergences.notes: File exists at actual path; sha256 confirmed match in Stream B. Manifest entry is incorrect — same prefix mismatch as DIV-001. Both CHART_FACTS files share the same wrong-prefix bug in the manifest.

divergences.description: Three ephemeris CSV source files are present in FILE_REGISTRY_v1_14.md and confirmed in GCS (set_C), but absent from CAPABILITY_MANIFEST.json (the new governance tool from Phase 1B).
divergences.downstream_evidence: Streams F, G, H all PASS: ephemeris_daily (660,726 rows), eclipses (913 rows), retrogrades (2,462 rows), sade_sati_phases (46 rows) — all content-faithful to CSV sources
divergences.notes: FILE_REGISTRY is SUPERSEDED (2026-04-27) but was the correct tool at upload time. Manifest was not back-populated with CSV entries when it replaced FILE_REGISTRY. No data loss; the CSVs are in GCS and their downstream Postgres tables pass full audit.

informational_findings.description: GCS contains one extra object not in manifest: gs://madhav-marsys-sources/00_ARCHITECTURE/VALIDATED_ASSET_REGISTRY_v1_0.json. This is a governance artifact uploaded during Phase 14 asset validation. No remediation required; manifest need not track every governance file.

chart_facts schema audit spec referenced columns 'fact_category' and 'fact_type'; actual schema uses 'category' (no 'fact_type' column). Data is fully present; this is a column-naming difference between the pre-migration spec and the loader output. No remediation required.

ephemeris_daily computation parameters: midnight UT (hour=0.0) rather than the commonly assumed noon, and TRUE_NODE for Rahu/Ketu rather than MEAN_NODE. These are calibration parameters confirmed by reverse-engineering against birth-date rows. Not divergences — the values are internally consistent and pyswisseph determinism check passed with max delta 0.00000145 degrees. Two transliteration variants in nakshatra column: 'Moola'='Mula', 'Dhanishta'='Dhanishtha'.

l25_msr_signals: signal_number column max=496 while total row count=499 and COUNT(DISTINCT signal_id)=499. The signal_number column has a gap (not contiguous through 499). This is not a migration fault — the 499-row count matches spec exactly. signal_number should not be used as a proxy for canonical set size.
